alert_summary: Branch sites reporting high latency and packet loss toward the datacenter.

alert_timeline.message: BGP session instability detected — route withdrawals observed
ISP circuit packet loss above 3% threshold
Latency spike on external peering path

candidate_ranking.reason: WAN handoff is the common upstream dependency for all impacted nodes
Peripheral node — no direct evidence of involvement
On the propagation path between WAN-PE-01 and WAN-ISP-01
First observed symptom — downstream impact evidence points here
Downstream node reachable from root — impacted rather than causal
Downstream node reachable from root — impacted rather than causal

reasoning_steps: 1. First symptom observed at WAN-PE-01 — router reports connectivity degradation.
2. Alert propagation traced upstream: WAN-ISP-01 → WAN-MPLS-CORE → WAN-PE-01.
3. WAN-ISP-01 has highest topological reach — zero or minimal in-degree, maximum out-degree.
4. All impacted nodes (WAN-MPLS-CORE, WAN-PE-01, WAN-PE-02…) are downstream-reachable from WAN-ISP-01.
5. Candidate ranking confirms WAN-ISP-01 as primary root cause (score: 0.85).
recommended_actions: Check MPLS circuit utilization and carrier status page
Verify BGP session state and routing table completeness
Test failover path reachability from affected branch sites
Contact carrier if physical layer errors are detected
